content: {"key_findings": ["Round 2 retrieved evidence touching the AHA 2024 NHANES signal (Zhong et al.), which is now grounded in fetched sources rather than absent as in Round 1", "Regulatory perspective improved: FTC (5x) and EFSA (4x) references now in the corpus, with FDA context emerging", "GLP-1 head-to-head context (1x) and cost-effectiveness signals are partial but not yet sufficient for confident comparative analysis", "Mechanistic depth (IL-22/ILC3, circadian, autophagy) remains strong from Round 1 and needs no additional querying", "Remaining gaps: Ramadan pooled cardiovascular outcomes systematic review text, LMIC-specific trials, human mechanistic studies of circadian/mTOR beyond animal models"], "perspective_gaps": ["Regional", "Methodological"], "web_queries": ["Ramadan diurnal intermittent fasting cardiovascular pooled meta-analysis systematic review Muslim adults", "LMIC low middle income country intermittent fasting metabolic syndrome trial South Asia Africa", "human circadian clock gene expression intermittent fasting clinical trial chronotype"], "academic_queries": ["Ramadan fasting cardiovascular mortality systematic review meta-analysis", "intermittent fasting randomized controlled trial 12 months long duration sustained adherence"], "exa_queries": ["intermittent fasting metabolic syndrome sub-Saharan Africa South Asia clinical trial"], "convergence_assessment": "narrowing", "should_continue": true, "reasoning": "Round 2 closed the largest Round 1 gaps (Regulatory, Public Health long-term safety). Round 3 should be the last expansive round, targeting Regional (Ramadan pooled outcomes, LMIC trials) and Methodological depth. After Round 3 the pipeline should converge to avoid diminishing returns.", "knowledge_gaps": ["Ramadan systematic review pooled cardiovascular and metabolic endpoint effect sizes with explicit Muslim adult denominators", "LMIC trials of IF in metabolic syndrome with region-specific outcomes", "Long-duration (>12 month) prospective cohort IF studies", "Human (not animal) mechanistic studies of circadian/mTOR/autophagy pathways under IF"]}
